sanskrit: आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत्। तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी॥
transliteration: āpūryamāṇam acala-pratiṣṭhaṁ samudram āpaḥ praviśanti yadvat | tadvat kāmā yaṁ praviśanti sarve sa śāntim āpnoti na kāma-kāmī ||
literal_meaning: Just as waters enter the ever-filled, immovable ocean, so do all desires enter that person; he attains peace, not the one who desires objects of desire.
contextual_meaning: Desires naturally arise, but inner peace depends on remaining unshaken like the ocean rather than frantically trying to satisfy every want.
psychological_state: Secure-autonomous attachment to Self; reduced limbic reactivity to appetitive cues.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media at 1 a.m., feeling FOMO, yet choosing to put the phone down and sleep instead of chasing every trending dopamine hit.
anchor_text: Let desires flow in without disturbing your inner stillness.
